skill.name: coding-standards
skill.description: React 19 and TypeScript coding standards for Portfolio Buddy 2, covering components, hooks, typing, testing, imports, and reviews.
skill.summary: Apply React 19 and TypeScript standards for Portfolio Buddy 2 code work.
skill.author: 5MinFutures

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command and weak cryptography patterns, but review found they are false positives from Markdown code fences, inline TypeScript identifiers, and ordinary example text. No executable scripts, network calls, credential access, prompt injection, or malicious intent were found in SKILL.md. The skill is safe to publish as documentation-only coding guidance.

low_findings.title: False positive external command detections
low_findings.description: The external command detections occur inside Markdown code fences and inline TypeScript examples, such as component structure, hooks, imports, and commit-message formatting. They are documentation examples and are not executed by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are Markdown examples and headings, not runnable skill code. No shell invocation, dynamic execution, or instruction to run commands is present.

low_findings.title: False positive weak cryptography detections
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections are caused by unrelated text fragments in the description and TypeScript examples. No cryptographic API, hash function, encryption routine, or password handling logic appears in the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines contain prose or ordinary test/example code, not crypto usage. There is no evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, or similar weak algorithms being used.

low_findings.title: False positive reconnaissance detections
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance detections map to TypeScript sample code and documentation text, including array find examples and error handling examples. They do not gather system information or inspect a host environment.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed content is static coding guidance with no operating system commands or environment inspection. No evidence of host reconnaissance was found.

content.value_statement: Frontend teams need consistent React and TypeScript patterns across repeated code work. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code concrete standards for Portfolio Buddy 2 components, hooks, tests, imports, and reviews.
actual_capabilities: Guides React 19 component structure with hooks, memoization, and stable callbacks.
Defines TypeScript standards for strict typing, null checks, inference, and avoiding any.
Documents component size limits and refactoring targets for Portfolio Buddy 2 files.
Specifies file organization, naming conventions, import ordering, and commit message format.
Outlines testing priorities for calculations, parsing, hooks, edge cases, and error states.
Provides a review checklist for TypeScript, component size, errors, comments, and Chart.js usage.
limitations: The guidance is tailored to Portfolio Buddy 2 and may need changes for other projects.
It does not run linters, tests, formatters, or automated refactoring tools by itself.
Some listed violations are project-specific snapshots and may become outdated.
It is documentation only and does not include reusable scripts or templates.

use_cases.title: Create Consistent Components
use_cases.description: Use the standards while building new React components with typed props, clear state, and predictable hook usage.

use_cases.title: Review Portfolio Buddy Changes
use_cases.description: Check pull requests against the documented TypeScript, error handling, testing, and import rules.

use_cases.title: Plan Focused Refactors
use_cases.description: Identify oversized components and move logic into smaller components, hooks, or utilities.

prompt_templates.title: Check a Component
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of one React component.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this React component using the coding-standards skill. Focus on props, hooks, TypeScript types, error handling, and component size.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Type Safety
prompt_templates.scenario: Find and reduce unsafe TypeScript patterns.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the coding-standards skill to identify weak typing in this file. Suggest specific replacements for any, unsafe null access, and unclear inferred types.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor an Oversized Component
prompt_templates.scenario: Split a large component into focused pieces.
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the coding-standards skill to refactor this oversized component. Propose extracted components, custom hooks, utilities, and the final responsibility of each piece.

prompt_templates.title: Perform a Full Standards Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review across a feature branch.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit these changes against the coding-standards skill. Cover React 19 patterns, TypeScript strictness, Chart.js usage, tests, imports, comments, and the review checklist.

output_examples.input: Review a metrics table component for standards compliance.
output: The component follows the hook order rule and uses typed props.
The sort callback should use a stable callback because it is passed to child headers.
The file is above the preferred size limit, so extract row rendering into a focused child component.

output_examples.input: Plan a refactor for a large portfolio section.
output: Keep orchestration in the parent component.
Move contract multiplier state into a custom hook.
Extract chart rendering and portfolio statistics into separate components with typed props.

output_examples.input: Check a data parsing utility before merging.
output: Add explicit return types for parsed trade records.
Handle empty input and malformed rows as test cases.
Use comments only where the calculation intent is not obvious.

best_practices: Use the checklist before merge so standards are applied consistently.
Pair refactoring suggestions with small, reviewable changes.
Update project-specific violation counts when the codebase changes.
anti_patterns: Do not treat the standards as a replacement for running tests and type checks.
Do not apply Portfolio Buddy 2 file structure rules blindly to unrelated projects.
Do not add broad abstractions unless they reduce clear duplication or complexity.

faq.question: What projects is this skill designed for?
faq.answer: It is designed for Portfolio Buddy 2, a React 19 and TypeScript frontend project.

faq.question: Can it enforce standards automatically?
faq.answer: No. It gives review and implementation guidance, but it does not run tooling or modify code alone.

faq.question: Does it support Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What React patterns does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers functional components, hooks, derived state, stable callbacks, memoization, and component extraction.

faq.question: Does it include testing guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It prioritizes calculations, data transformations, error states, edge cases, and hook return values.

faq.question: Is the skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. Security review found documentation-only content and no confirmed dangerous behavior.
